headline: Student competition: conflict resolution ideas
body_text:
This article with recommendations by students is part of the Financial Times free schools access programme. Details/registration here.

Entries are open for the 2025-26 FT competition with the Crimson Global Foundation on contemporary conflicts and how to solve them.

Secondary and high school students around the world are invited to research, analyse and propose solutions to any form of current conflict in the world, which could be geopolitical, economic, corporate or cultural.

The winners have the chance to stand out in university and job applications, with the chance of publication in the FT and to share their views and ideas with others and contribute to debates on important topics.

In the first stage, students should register on the competition website, and by January 15 2026 select three FT stories, post the links in the comments field beneath this article, add a brief explanation of why they have been chosen and then share the link via the competition website form.
